report.title: OpenCode Desktop App — UI/UX Surface & Design System Audit
report.methodology: Read-only codebase audit of packages/ui, packages/app, packages/storybook, packages/desktop across 100+ files, 5 parallel research subagents

technologies.styling: Tailwind CSS v4 + CSS custom properties (design tokens) + component-level CSS files
technologies.icon_system: Custom SVG sprite with 109+ icons, injected <symbol>/<use> pattern
technologies.color_engine: Oklch color space, seed-based algorithmic palette generation
technologies.storybook: Storybook v10 with addon-a11y, addon-docs, addon-vitest
technologies.font_loading: Inter (sans) + JetBrainsMono Nerd Font Mono via @font-face
technologies.testing: vitest (storybook-addon-vitest), no dedicated component test suite

design_system.token_architecture: Oklch-based seed color → algorithmic palette generation → 300+ resolved CSS custom properties across 11 token categories (background, surface, text, border, icon, button, input, syntax, markdown, avatar, diff)

theme_details.custom_themes: Loadable from URL, registerable via registerTheme() API
theme_details.runtime: ThemeProvider context → import.meta.glob → resolveThemeVariant() → themeToCss() → inline <style> injection with `:root { --var: value }`, localStorage CSS cache for instant reload

strengths: Kobalte headless primitives provide built-in ARIA, keyboard navigation, and focus management across ALL components
Consistent composability pattern: all components accept `class`, `classList`, `children`, use `splitProps` for clean prop separation
Compound component pattern (Object.assign) on Accordion, Tabs, DropdownMenu, Toast, Collapsible, Card — flexible slot-based composition
Semantic variant system across Button (primary/secondary/ghost), Card (normal/error/warning/success/info), Dialog (normal/large/x-large), Tabs (normal/alt/pill/settings)
Sophisticated Card component with auto-icon selection per variant and CSS variable accent injection
Custom SVG sprite icon system is lightweight, self-contained, and performant (<use> avoids DOM duplication)
Toast system with showToast/showPromiseToast API, progress tracks, icons, action buttons, persistent mode
Rich Tooltip with TooltipKeybind variant showing keyboard shortcut pills
RadioGroup supports generic typed options with value/label projection functions
Select component supports grouping (groupBy), keyboard navigation, portal rendering
gaps: Two parallel design systems (v1 and v2) — some duplication in icon sets (95 v1 icons + 14 v2 icons), CSS files, and component variants
MessagePart component at 2,414 lines is a monolith combining message rendering, tool display, copy logic, animation, and event handling — violates single-responsibility
MessagePart exports 27 public symbols including internal implementation details
No Slider component, DatePicker, or ColorPicker — limited form input variety
No dedicated test suite for UI components (only storybook-addon-vitest and a few .test.ts files)
Transition durations vary arbitrarily (85ms, 120ms, 140ms, 150ms, 180ms, 200ms, 220ms, 250ms, 480ms) — no centralized timing scale
v2 Tailwind colors auto-generated by script — hard to understand origin of token values
No focus trap directive for modals (Kobalte handles dialog focus, but there's no generic FocusTrap component)
No portal API exposed outside of Kobalte (Select, Tooltip, Popover, DropdownMenu each portal internally but no standalone Portal component)

rigid_components.name: message-part.tsx
rigid_components.why: 2,414-line monolith with 94 top-level symbols, 27 public exports, manages text rendering, tool display, code highlighting, animations, copy logic, event handling, session navigation — should be 5+ separate files

rigid_components.name: prompt-input.tsx
rigid_components.why: 2,172-line monolith with 72 top-level symbols — manages editor, history, popovers, file attachments, agent/model selectors, keyboard shortcuts, drag/drop — very high cognitive load

developer_experience.error_evidence: ErrorPage component formats structured error chains recursively, handles 9+ error types with specific actionable messages (ProviderModelNotFoundError includes 'Did you mean' suggestions), offers restart/report/export-logs/check-updates. ErrorBoundary captures unhandled errors + Sentry integration. Toast system used for 72+ operation failures with variant styling (default/success/error/loading).
developer_experience.loading_evidence: Spinner (custom SVG grid animation), Skeleton (pulsing placeholders for home and sidebar), Progress (track/fill bar), ProgressCircle (compact circular), Toast with progress track, showPromiseToast auto-transitions loading→success/error. Suspense boundaries at app root and server connection gate.
developer_experience.shortcut_evidence: Editable keybinds via settings-keybinds.tsx with live key capture, conflict detection, and macOS/Windows key display. 6 groups: General, Session, Navigation, Model & Agent, Terminal, Prompt. Editable keybinds include terminal.toggle, terminal.new, file.attach. Default palette: mod+shift+p.
developer_experience.search_evidence: Command palette (DialogCommandPalette) with fuzzysort filtering, category grouping, keyboard navigation, keybind hints, empty state. Home page session search with filter by time/agent/model.

options: Sound effects profile (3 dropdowns with preview)

developer_experience.onboarding_detail: 4-step DialogOnboarding: Welcome → Connect Provider (6 provider cards) → Quick Tips → You're All Set. Arrow key navigation, step indicators, persist-onboarded flag.

delight_factors.animation_detail: Spring physics (useSpring) for tool panel expand/collapse, text reveal (700ms mask wipe with spring cubic-bezier), text strikethrough, tool status title width, animated number count. CSS keyframes for pulse, shimmer (text-shimmer gradient sweep), fade-up (staggered up to 30 children), session progress whip (clip-path animation), thinking heading 4 variants (spring-up/down/pop/blur). Toast pop-in/out/swipe on v2 components. prefers-reduced-motion respected in 9 CSS files.
delight_factors.micro_detail: Comprehensive hover effects via overlay gradient system on all v2 interactive components (buttons, icon buttons, inputs, switches, checkboxes, radio buttons, select, tabs). Strong :focus-visible 2px outline with 2.5px offset on all interactive elements. Active/pressed state via overlay on all button variants. Checkbox has SVG checkmark animation. Switch has smooth thumb slide.
delight_factors.icon_detail: 109 custom SVG icons in consistent outline style, stroke="currentColor", stroke-linecap="square". Navigation icons have active/inactive variants for visual state. 4 size tiers (small/normal/medium/large). SVG sprite injection into DOM body for performance. No external icon library dependencies.

typography.font_stack: System font stack with Inter (loaded) and JetBrainsMono Nerd Font Mono (loaded). User-customizable sans/mono/terminal font families.
typography.markdown_readability: Headings same size but color-distinguished, tables scrollable, code blocks with copy buttons, external links open in new tab with noopener/noreferrer, inline code URLs become clickable links

native_feel.titlebar_detail: Custom titlebar on all platforms (titleBarStyle: 'hidden'). macOS: traffic light spacing (12x, 14y). Windows: frame: false with custom titleBarOverlay and zoom-aware controls. Linux: default platform frame. No vibrancy/transparency/glass effects on any platform.
native_feel.zoom_support: Pinch zoom with clampZoom(0.2-10), zoom-aware titlebar height scaling and Windows control width adjustment

notes: zh.ts has ~89 fewer keys than en.ts (874 vs 963)
Several locales have untranslated English fallback strings for session export keys
No comprehensive parity test (only 2 keys checked across 17 locales)
UI and app have separate i18n systems with merge bridge — potential for drift
Pluralization is convention-based (.one/.other/.many keys) — no CLDR plural rules
Date/number formatting uses Intl directly in 2 components instead of through i18n

strengths: World-class design token system with Oklch-based algorithmic palette generation — industry-leading approach used by few apps
38 built-in themes with light/dark/system support — competitive with VS Code and Faradian-level customization
Comprehensive accessibility via Kobalte (ARIA, keyboard nav, focus management on every component)
Consistent, composable component API with data-component/data-slot conventions across all 48+ components
Superb error handling with actionable error messages, Sentry integration, and formatted error chain display
70+ editable keyboard shortcuts with conflict detection and discoverable key hint overlay
Rich markdown rendering with Shiki syntax highlighting (200+ languages), KaTeX math, code copy, GFM
Smooth spring-based animations across tool panels, text reveal, and thinking heading transitions
109 custom SVG icons with consistent design language — no external icon debt
Comprehensive onboarding (4-step), empty states, loading states, and error boundaries
18-locale i18n with dynamic loading and template interpolation
Storybook with a11y addon, docs, vitest support
weaknesses: MessagePart (2,414 lines) and PromptInput (2,172 lines) are monolithic components with excessive responsibility
Two parallel design systems (v1 and v2) create inconsistency — dual icon sets, CSS files, component variants
No comprehensive translation parity — zh.ts missing 89+ keys, several locales have English fallback strings
No CLDR pluralization support, no i18n-based date/number formatting — uses Intl directly in 2 components
No dedicated component test suite beyond Storybook — risk of regressions on refactoring
Transition durations are inconsistent (85ms to 480ms) with no centralized timing scale or design rationale
Missing form components: Slider, DatePicker, ColorPicker
No design system documentation site outside Storybook — tokens and patterns are distributed across code
No emoji picker or reaction system for messages
No vibrancy/transparency/glass effects on desktop — solid background only
Linux desktop uses platform default frame — no custom titlebar optimization
MessagePart exports 27 public symbols including internal implementation details — no clean public API boundary
recommendations: Extract MessagePart into 5+ focused components: MessageContainer, TextPart, ToolPart, ReasoningPart, MessageDivider — each under 300 lines
Complete v2 migration: consolidate to a single design system, remove duplicate v1 icon set and CSS, standardize on v2 component variants
Add comprehensive i18n parity test that checks all 1,117 keys across all 18 locales, with CI enforcement
Add CLDR pluralization via Intl.PluralRules and date formatting via Intl.DateTimeFormat channels in i18n
Add component-level visual regression tests (vitest storybook-addon-vitest is a good start)
Add centralized animation timing scale with 3-5 canonical durations (e.g., instant=85ms, fast=150ms, normal=250ms, slow=400ms)
Add missing input components: Slider, DatePicker, ColorPicker (Kobalte has these primitives available)
Create a design system documentation artifact (Storybook is the right tool, add token/tier documentation)
Add emoji picker component for message reactions — enables collaborative agent feedback
Add macOS vibrancy (NSVisualEffectView) and Windows acrylic for native feel parity with VS Code
Add Linux custom titlebar with GTK headerbar-style integration for platform parity
Refactor message-part.tsx public API to export only 5-6 consumer-facing symbols
Spin up the design token schema as a visual dashboard (ThemeProvider already exposes all tools)

assessments.design_system_maturity: The theme engine (Oklch, 38 themes, URL loading, preview/commit) is at the level of VS Code, Linear, or Raycast — truly industry-leading.
assessments.component_api_quality: Consistent, clean, composable. The Object.assign compound pattern matches Radix/Solid-Headless conventions. Props follow a predictable pattern across all components.
assessments.animation_quality: Smooth and deliberate. Spring physics on key interactions is a mark of serious attention to detail. (Reduces to 'basic' vs the truly cinematic feel of Linear or Apple Music.)
assessments.accessibility: Strong foundation via Kobalte, but no dedicated a11y testing beyond Storybook addon-a11y — risk of regressions.
assessments.largest_drag_factor: Two parallel design systems (v1/v2) — until migration completes, the app has 2 component libraries, 2 CSS systems, 2 icon sets, and 2 animation systems living side by side.
assessments.path_to_industry_leading: Resolve the v1/v2 split, extract the monoliths, add design docs, layer in a11y regression testing, and add the missing value-tier features (emoji, native vibrancy, date formatting). The foundation is already world-class.
